question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 rotates the OSPF MD5 key on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (R3↔R2, Area 0)
from `cisco123` to `wrongkey` without coordinating with R2, which keeps
`cisco123`. OSPF Hellos signed with the new key fail R2's HMAC
verification (and vice-versa). The adjacency drops out of FULL with
authentication errors logged on the receiving side. Classic key-rotation
outage when one router is forgotten in the rollout.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.103 (R3), the OSPF MD5 key on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R3<->R2 link, area 0) was rotated to `wrongkey` while R2 still uses `cisco123`.
The two ends now disagree on the MD5 key, so each side's HMAC verification of the other's OSPF packets fails; OSPF cannot maintain an authenticated adjacency on that link and routes stop being exchanged across it.
Fix: restore the matching MD5 key (cisco123) on R3's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R1: neighbor 192.0.0.6.
- R2: neighbor 192.0.0.5.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R1 (1 instance):
- R1: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready

OSPF adjacency state change (%ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG):
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "dead timer expired".
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "dead timer expired".
- Bilateral: both ends (R2, R3) report the transition.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 message-digest-key 1 md5 wrongkey

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 message-digest-key 1 md5 cisco123

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:43:57.187 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.0.6 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:43:57.189 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.0.5 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 65001)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:43:57.194 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to Not NSR-Ready

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:44:06.422 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: dead timer expired, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:44:02.356 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: dead timer expired, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000
